compiled_state.title: Commit-level source attribution via git-native trailers
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Plugin maintainers triaging post-deploy regressions today must cross-reference session transcript logs — which expire or get compacted — to identify which sub-agent authored a suspect change. There is no durable, queryable record attached to the commit itself, forcing manual error-prone archaeology and making automated auditing impossible. By accumulating a contributor list in the session artifacts dir and emitting it as git-native trailers at commit time, every DSO-produced commit carries its own provenance chain, queryable via standard git log without leaving the terminal. The trailer set resets after each successful commit so per-commit attribution stays accurate across a multi-commit session.

## Success Criteria
- SC-0: A plugin maintainer investigating any DSO-produced commit can identify all contributing agents and the triggering skill within one git command, without opening any log file or session transcript.
- SC-1: When attribution.enabled=true, commits produced by /dso:commit include DSO trailers appended via git interpret-trailers. Scalar trailers (Jira-Ticket, DSO-Task, DSO-Story, DSO-Epic, DSO-Review-Score) included only when non-empty; DSO-Task/Story/Epic resolved by traversing the active task's parent chain upward, emitting each level only when that ticket type exists; DSO-Review-Score read from $ARTIFACTS_DIR/reviewer-findings.json score field. Multi-value trailers (DSO-Agent, DSO-Skill, DSO-Model) emit one line per unique value accumulated since the last commit.
- SC-2: attribution.enabled is opt-in via dso-config.conf (default: false).
- SC-3: For any attributed commit in a multi-agent sprint session, git log --format="%(trailers:key=DSO-Agent,valueonly)" returns at least two distinct non-empty lines, one per unique agent type that contributed.
- SC-4: When no contributor records exist and no scalar values resolve, the commit message contains no DSO trailer lines and /dso:commit exits 0.
- SC-5: A test asserts that when attribution produces trailers, the lines before the final blank-line separator contain no strings matching ^(Jira-Ticket|DSO-[A-Za-z-]+): — only the trailer block following the separator does. Commits with no body (subject-only) also satisfy this constraint.
- SC-6: After git commit exits 0, $ARTIFACTS_DIR/attribution-contributors.jsonl exists and has size 0. If truncation fails, /dso:commit emits a warning to stderr but does not fail.
- SC-7: When git <2.6 is detected, TRAILER_SKIPPED is emitted to stderr, attribution is skipped, and /dso:commit continues. A test verifies this by injecting a mock git version string below 2.6 and asserting TRAILER_SKIPPED on stderr with no DSO trailer lines in the commit message.

## Dependencies
- Soft interaction with 6d90-93d5 (Conventional Commits) — both modify /dso:commit; not a hard sequencing dependency
- 1083-fb3d (PR-based merge) depends on Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task trailer infrastructure delivered here — this epic should reach done before 1083-fb3d enters sprint

## Approach
Introduce $ARTIFACTS_DIR/attribution-contributors.jsonl as a session-scoped append-only log. A PostToolUse hook on the Agent tool (adds a branch to the existing post-agent.sh dispatcher) captures .tool_input.subagent_type and model; a PostToolUse hook on the Skill tool (new post-skill.sh dispatcher) captures skill names by reading .tool_input.skill. Both append entries to the same JSONL log. The commit workflow reads the log, deduplicates by field, constructs --trailer flags for git interpret-trailers, and pipes the final commit message through before passing to git commit. Scalar values sourced separately: Jira-Ticket from SYNC event jira_key, DSO-Task/Story/Epic via parent traversal, DSO-Review-Score from $ARTIFACTS_DIR/reviewer-findings.json score field. File truncated on git commit exit 0. attribution.enabled defaults to false.

## Scenario Analysis
- Concurrent JSONL writes from parallel agent completions (high, runtime): non-atomic shell appends corrupt the JSONL; commit either drops lines or parse-errors
- JSONL dirty on failed commit (medium, runtime): re-accumulated contributors appear in next commit's trailers across change boundaries
- Nested Agent tool calls inflate DSO-Agent list (medium, runtime): sub-sub-agents both appended to orchestrator's log
- Trailer injection splits existing trailer blocks (high, runtime): Signed-off-by or Co-authored-by present in message; blank-line boundary misdetection inlines DSO trailers into body
- Truncation/write race on rapid sequential commits (medium, runtime): hook write for commit N+1 wiped by truncation from commit N
- Vendor-patched git version string misparsed (medium, deployment): 2.39.3-1.el9 suffix causes TRAILER_SKIPPED on capable git install
- ARTIFACTS_DIR absent in CI (high, deployment): hooks write to wrong path silently; commit proceeds with no trailers, no error signal
- post-agent.sh regression when attribution disabled (critical, deployment): unconditional code path breaks all Agent dispatches for all users
- Review gate hash mismatch from message mutation (critical, deployment): trailer injection after review hash recorded causes gate to flag mismatch on next commit
- JSONL written to repo root when ARTIFACTS_DIR unset (high, configuration): file staged and committed as tracked source, leaking attribution history into repo
- Stale DSO-Review-Score from previous review cycle (medium, configuration): docs-only commit inherits score from unrelated prior review
- Unbounded JSONL growth on repeated commit failures (medium, configuration): hundreds of accumulated entries produce impractical trailer block on eventual success

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered — 12 scenarios surviving blue team filter
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md); bot-psychologist skipped — purely mechanical pipeline steps, no behavioral guidance changes

comments.body: PREPLANNING_CONTEXT_LIGHTWEIGHT: {"version":1,"epicId":"170b-2ac9","generatedBy":"preplanning-lightweight","epic":{"title":"Commit-level source attribution via git-native trailers","doneDefinitions":["When attribution is enabled, commits made via /dso:commit contain DSO-Skill, DSO-Agent, DSO-Task, DSO-Story, DSO-Epic, DSO-Model, and DSO-Review-Score trailers (each present only when its source value is non-empty)","The attribution.enabled config key in dso-config.conf controls whether trailers are appended (default: false)","Standard git trailer queries return the correct skill name for attributed commits","Commits without resolvable context omit all DSO trailers and /dso:commit completes successfully","Trailers conform to git trailer block convention (blank line separator, no pollution of subject or body)","Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic"],"considerations":["[Reliability] git interpret-trailers version compatibility \u2014 graceful degradation when git <2.6","[Reliability] Stale active-skill-context \u2014 validate session currency before reading","[Reliability] Trailer value sanitization \u2014 colons/newlines in ticket titles","[Testing] CI parser interaction \u2014 commit-lint/release-please compatibility","[Testing] Portability lint interaction \u2014 use short names not absolute paths","[Maintainability] active-agent-context is new \u2014 needs format documentation"]},"stories":[]}
comments.author: Claude
